* Each color needed for the tiles required 192 units of material, since 48 tiles multiplied by 4 colors equals 192.  
* The painter needed 20 bottles of each color, as 192 divided by 10 gives 19.2, which rounds up to 20.  

The process of calculating the required materials was a bit of a challenge, but it was nothing compared to the hell of trying to figure out how to evenly distribute the colors. The painter had to be precise, like the palindrome "level," to ensure the fresco looked perfect.
